prompt:
The following Lean 4 file contains a partial strong normalization proof for a first-order rewrite calculus. Three of eight rule cases are proved; the remaining five are marked sorry. Provide a proof skeleton for completing the remaining cases. Do not modify the file or run the compiler. Do not read any other files in the workspace or search the web.

<workspace>\test-files\Test-02-Completion-Nat-Lex.lean
response:
Here are paste-in proof skeletons for the five remaining `sorry` branches in
. They follow the same `drop_left` / `drop_right` pattern already used.

```lean
-- integrate (delta t) --> void
| _, _, Step.R_int_delta t => by
    by_cases hk0 : kappa t = 0
    · have hmu : mu void < mu (integrate (delta t)) := by
        simp [mu]
      have hk : kappa void = kappa (integrate (delta t)) := by
        simp [kappa, hk0]
      exact drop_right hmu hk
    · have hkpos : 0 < kappa t := Nat.pos_of_ne_zero hk0
      have hk : kappa void < kappa (integrate (delta t)) := by
        simpa [kappa] using hkpos
      exact drop_left hk
```

```lean
-- recDelta b s void --> b
| _, _, Step.R_rec_zero b s => by
    -- Always: mu(b) < mu(recDelta b s void)
    have hmu : mu b < mu (recDelta b s void) := by
      simp [mu]; omega

    -- For kappa: either it ties (then use mu), or it strictly drops (then use kappa).
    -- Split on which branch of the max controls kappa(recDelta b s void).
    cases Nat.le_total (kappa s) (kappa b) with
    | inl hs_le =>
        -- kappa(recDelta ...) = kappa b, so use drop_right via mu
        have hk : kappa b = kappa (recDelta b s void) := by
          -- simp should reduce kappa(recDelta b s void) to max (max kb ks) 0,
          -- then use hs_le to collapse max kb ks to kb.
          -- (You may need to hand-hold simp with Nat.max_eq_left / Nat.max_eq_right.)
          simp [kappa, hs_le, Nat.max_eq_left, Nat.max_eq_right, Nat.zero_le]
        exact drop_right hmu hk
    | inr hb_le =>
        -- Here kb ≤ ks; refine further into kb < ks (drop_left) or kb = ks (drop_right).
        cases lt_or_eq_of_le hb_le with
        | inl hk_lt =>
            have hk : kappa b < kappa (recDelta b s void) := by
              -- show kappa(recDelta ...) = kappa s, then rewrite to hk_lt
              -- (again: simp + Nat.max_eq_right + Nat.zero_le)
              simpa [kappa, hb_le, Nat.max_eq_left, Nat.max_eq_right, Nat.zero_le] using hk_lt
            exact drop_left hk
        | inr hk_eq =>
            have hk : kappa b = kappa (recDelta b s void) := by
              -- if kb = ks, then kappa(recDelta ...) collapses to kb
              simp [kappa, hk_eq, Nat.max_eq_left, Nat.max_eq_right, Nat.zero_le]
            exact drop_right hmu hk
```

```lean
-- recDelta b s (delta n) --> app s (recDelta b s n)
| _, _, Step.R_rec_succ b s n => by
    -- Intended route: strict drop on kappa (source has +1, target doesn't).
    -- Let m be the “base” max that appears in both.
    let m : Nat := Nat.max (Nat.max (kappa b) (kappa s)) (kappa n)

    have hk_src : kappa (recDelta b s (delta n)) = m + 1 := by
      simp [kappa, kappa_rec_delta, m]

    -- You want: kappa(app s (recDelta b s n)) = m.
    -- This is straightforward if you can establish:
    --   hk_rec : kappa (recDelta b s n) = m
    -- However, if `n` can be `delta _`, then `kappa (recDelta b s n)` may also have a `+1`,
    -- and the measure may fail to strictly decrease without an additional well-formedness invariant.
    -- Skeleton: split on `n` and close each constructor case by simp/max-arithmetic.
    have hk_rec : kappa (recDelta b s n) = m := by
      -- cases n <;> simp [kappa, m, Nat.max_assoc, Nat.max_left_comm, Nat.max_comm]
      -- (fill in any remaining max normalization)
      sorry

    have hk_tgt : kappa (app s (recDelta b s n)) = m := by
      -- since m = max(max kb ks) kn, we have kappa s ≤ m
      have hs_le_m : kappa s ≤ m := by
        -- m = max (max kb ks) kn
        -- kappa s ≤ max kb ks ≤ max (max kb ks) kn
        exact Nat.le_trans (Nat.le_max_right _ _) (Nat.le_max_left _ _)
      -- and with hk_rec, the max collapses to m
      simp [kappa, hk_rec, m, Nat.max_eq_right hs_le_m]

    have hk : kappa (app s (recDelta b s n)) < kappa (recDelta b s (delta n)) := by
      -- m < m+1
      simpa [hk_tgt, hk_src] using Nat.lt_succ_self m

    exact drop_left hk
```

```lean
-- eqW a a --> void
| _, _, Step.R_eq_refl a => by
    by_cases hk0 : kappa a = 0
    · have hmu : mu void < mu (eqW a a) := by
        simp [mu]
      have hk : kappa void = kappa (eqW a a) := by
        simp [kappa, hk0]
      exact drop_right hmu hk
    · have hkpos : 0 < kappa a := Nat.pos_of_ne_zero hk0
      have hk : kappa void < kappa (eqW a a) := by
        -- kappa(eqW a a) = kappa a
        simpa [kappa] using hkpos
      exact drop_left hk
```

```lean
-- eqW a b --> integrate (merge a b)
| _, _, Step.R_eq_diff a b => by
    have hmu : mu (integrate (merge a b)) < mu (eqW a b) := by
      simp [mu]; omega
    have hk : kappa (integrate (merge a b)) = kappa (eqW a b) := by
      simp [kappa]
    exact drop_right hmu hk
```

If you want, I can also rewrite the `R_rec_succ` skeleton into a fully explicit `cases n` proof layout (one subgoal per constructor), so you can see exactly where the potential “`n = delta _`” subcase forces an extra invariant or a tweaked measure.
